meaning: n.森林地带,偏僻地区,荒野
examples: Rural residents are likely to find their lives in the boondocks too restrictive.
But if "Bridges to nowhere" start springing up in the boondocks, it will probably be money wasted.
These are educated and entrepreneurial people, says Schultz, and they are reinvigorating America's boondocks.
Some people would agonize over the decision to give up a thriving career and move out to the boondocks.
Discount plans may not be an option if you live in the boondocks.
